angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.

generated_text.quick_read: Current trout fishing conditions on the Soque River are fair to good with flows at 65.1 cfs and moderate gage height of 3.56 ft. Recent stocking supports the use of eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs for productive fishing.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is day 1 morning due to low wind speeds (0.6 mph average) and favorable sky cover around 20.5%, which supports comfortable fishing conditions and effective trout feeding activity.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS reports flow at 65.1 cfs, gage height at 3.56 ft, and water temperature is unavailable. Turbidity data is not available.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover is mostly light with averages near 21%, pressure data is unavailable, winds are light from the east averaging 2.2 mph with gusts up to 11.5 mph, and rain probability is low at 14%.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are expected to favor comfortable feeding lanes near cover under moderate light. Use clean drifts with smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors like Egg Pattern, Squirmy Worm, and Duracell nymphs. Present flies with caution as water temperature and pressure data are unavailable.
